general.normal: Your {test_name} is within normal range. This is good news.
general.slight_abnormal: Your {test_name} is slightly {direction}. Recommendation: monitor but no need for excessive worry.
general.moderate_abnormal: Your {test_name} shows moderate {direction}. Recommendation: consult a doctor for further evaluation.
general.significant_abnormal: Your {test_name} is significantly {direction}. Recommendation: see a doctor as soon as possible.
general.critical: Your {test_name} is at a critical level. Seek immediate medical attention!

low.mild: WBC count is mildly decreased, possibly caused by viral infection, medication effects, or mild nutritional deficiency.
low.moderate: WBC count is significantly decreased, immunity may be compromised, making you susceptible to infections. Recommendation: recheck and consult a doctor.
low.severe: WBC count is severely decreased, infection risk exists, medical intervention is needed.

high.mild: WBC count is mildly elevated, possibly indicating minor infection, stress response, or post-exercise state.
high.moderate: WBC count is significantly elevated, possibly indicating bacterial infection, inflammation, or tissue damage.
high.severe: WBC count is severely elevated, possibly indicating serious infection, leukemia, etc. Urgent evaluation needed.

low.mild: Mild anemia, may present with slight fatigue. Recommendation: increase iron-rich foods.
low.moderate: Moderate anemia, may present with dizziness, fatigue, and other symptoms. Recommendation: determine the cause and treat.
low.severe: Severe anemia, immediate medical intervention needed, may require blood transfusion.

high.mild: Hemoglobin is mildly elevated, possibly due to dehydration or high-altitude environment. Recommendation: increase water intake.
high.moderate: Hemoglobin is significantly elevated, polycythemia or other conditions may be present.
high.severe: Hemoglobin is severely elevated, blood viscosity is increased, thrombosis risk is high.

low.mild: Platelet count is mildly decreased, coagulation function is essentially normal, avoid trauma.
low.moderate: Platelet count is significantly decreased, bleeding risk is increased, avoid strenuous exercise and injury.
low.severe: Platelet count is severely decreased, bleeding risk is high, medical intervention is needed.

high.mild: Platelet count is mildly elevated, generally no special treatment needed.
high.moderate: Platelet count is significantly elevated, thrombosis risk is increased. Recommendation: further evaluation.
high.severe: Platelet count is severely elevated, thrombosis risk is very high, treatment is needed.

high.mild: Total cholesterol is slightly high, borderline elevated. Recommendation: adjust diet and increase physical activity.
high.moderate: Total cholesterol is significantly elevated, cardiovascular disease risk is increased. Recommendation: low-fat diet and recheck.
high.severe: Total cholesterol is severely elevated, medication and lifestyle changes are needed.

high.mild: LDL (bad) cholesterol is mildly elevated. Recommendation: reduce saturated fat intake.
high.moderate: LDL (bad) cholesterol is significantly elevated, atherosclerosis risk is increased.
high.severe: LDL (bad) cholesterol is severely elevated, medication is strongly recommended.

low.mild: HDL (good) cholesterol is slightly low. Recommendation: increase aerobic exercise.
low.moderate: HDL (good) cholesterol is significantly low, cardiovascular protective effect is diminished.
low.severe: HDL (good) cholesterol is severely insufficient, cardiovascular risk is increased.

high.mild: Triglycerides are mildly elevated. Recommendation: reduce sugar and refined carbohydrates.
high.moderate: Triglycerides are significantly elevated, pancreatitis risk is increased. Limit alcohol intake.
high.severe: Triglycerides are severely elevated, acute pancreatitis risk is high, medication is needed.

high.mild: ALT (transaminase) is mildly elevated, possibly caused by fatty liver, alcohol, or medication.
high.moderate: ALT (transaminase) is significantly elevated, indicating hepatocellular damage. Recommendation: check liver function and ultrasound.
high.severe: ALT (transaminase) is severely elevated, acute hepatitis or other serious liver disease may be present.

high.mild: AST is mildly elevated, possibly caused by muscle damage or mild liver disease.
high.moderate: AST is significantly elevated, need to differentiate between liver or myocardial damage.
high.severe: AST is severely elevated, serious liver damage or myocardial infarction may be present.

low.mild: Creatinine is mildly low, possibly due to low muscle mass or insufficient protein intake.

high.mild: Creatinine is mildly elevated, possibly indicating mild decline in kidney function.
high.moderate: Creatinine is significantly elevated, kidney function is impaired, nephrology evaluation needed.
high.severe: Creatinine is severely elevated, renal failure may be present, urgent treatment needed.

high.mild: Uric acid is mildly elevated. Increase water intake and reduce high-purine food consumption.
high.moderate: Uric acid is significantly elevated, gout risk is increased. Recommendation: low-purine diet.
high.severe: Uric acid is severely elevated, gout and kidney stone risk is high, medication is needed.

low.mild: Blood glucose is mildly low, possibly from prolonged fasting. Have something to eat.
low.moderate: Blood glucose is significantly low, hypoglycemic symptoms may occur. Carry candy with you.
low.severe: Blood glucose is severely low, immediate sugar supplementation needed.

high.mild: Fasting glucose is mildly elevated, in prediabetes range. Recommendation: dietary control and exercise.
high.moderate: Fasting glucose is significantly elevated, may indicate diabetes. Recommendation: further glucose tolerance testing.
high.severe: Fasting glucose is severely elevated, diabetes confirmed, standardized treatment needed.

high.mild: Glycated hemoglobin is mildly elevated, blood glucose control has been fair over the past 3 months.
high.moderate: Glycated hemoglobin is significantly elevated, blood glucose control has been poor recently. Treatment plan adjustment needed.
high.severe: Glycated hemoglobin is severely elevated, long-term poor glucose control, complication risk is high.

low.mild: TSH is mildly decreased, possible subclinical hyperthyroidism.
low.moderate: TSH is significantly decreased, possible hyperthyroidism. Recommendation: check thyroid hormones.
low.severe: TSH is severely decreased, hyperthyroidism is likely.

high.mild: TSH is mildly elevated, possible subclinical hypothyroidism.
high.moderate: TSH is significantly elevated, possible hypothyroidism. Recommendation: thyroid hormone supplementation.
high.severe: TSH is severely elevated, hypothyroidism is likely, treatment needed.

high.mild: CRP is mildly elevated, mild inflammation may be present.
high.moderate: CRP is significantly elevated, indicating inflammation or infection.
high.severe: CRP is severely elevated, serious infection or autoimmune disease may be present.

general.diet: Maintain a balanced diet, eat more vegetables and fruits, reduce processed foods.
general.exercise: At least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity aerobic exercise per week.
general.lifestyle: Maintain a regular schedule, avoid staying up late, quit smoking and limit alcohol.
general.followup: Recommendation: regular follow-up to monitor indicator trends.
general.medical: Recommendation: consult a specialist to develop a personalized treatment plan.

blood_routine.low_wbc: Pay attention to personal hygiene, avoid crowded places, prevent colds.
blood_routine.high_wbc: Get more rest, increase water intake. Seek medical attention promptly if fever occurs.
blood_routine.low_hgb: Eat more lean meat, animal liver, spinach, and other iron-rich foods.
blood_routine.low_plt: Avoid strenuous exercise, use a soft-bristled toothbrush, prevent injury.

lipid.high_ldl: Reduce animal fats, choose olive oil, eat more deep-sea fish.
lipid.high_tg: Limit alcohol, reduce refined sugar and sweets, control weight.
lipid.low_hdl: Increase aerobic exercise, quit smoking, moderate red wine consumption (if you already drink).

liver.high_alt: Abstain from alcohol, avoid hepatotoxic medications, regularly recheck liver function.
liver.fatty_liver: Control weight, low-fat diet, increase physical activity.

kidney.high_crea: Low-salt, low-protein diet, control blood pressure and blood glucose, avoid nephrotoxic medications.
kidney.high_ua: Increase water intake (at least 2000ml per day), reduce seafood and organ meat consumption.

diabetes.high_glucose: Control staple food portions, choose low glycemic index foods, walk after meals.
diabetes.monitor: Regularly monitor blood glucose, learn self-management of blood sugar.
